HAIL ESTIMATED TO BE JUST SHY OF GOLF BALL SIZED.
REPORTED 400 ACRES OF CROP DAMAGE DUE TO HAIL

A ROOF ON A BUILDING IN DOWNTOWN HAD A 30 BY 30 FOOT SECTION LIFTED AND BLOWN INTO THE BUILDING NEXT DOOR. THE HIGHEST MEASURED WIND GUSTS WERE AT THE GLENDIVE AIRPORT FROM THE SOUTH-SOUTHWEST AT 28 MPH GUSTING TO 38 MPH.

SAVAGE FIRE DEPARTMENT SURVEYED A METAL BUILDING THAT WAS SIGNIFICANTLY DAMAGED BY A MICROBURST. HALF THE ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF INTO NEARBY COTTONWOOD TREES. THE BUILDING WAS FACING SOUTH AND WINDS HIT FROM THE SOUTH. THERE WAS A LARGE OPENING AND THE WINDS ENTERED THE BUILDING AND BLEW OUT THE ROOF. WIND SPEEDS ARE ESTIMATED TO HAVE BEEN AROUND 80 MPH.
SNAPPED 2 INCH TREE BRANCHES OFF OF TREES AND PEA SIZED HAIL.

MEASURED BY RICHLAND COUNTY AGRIMET STATION. IN THIS VICINITY, 21 WOODEN POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED OFF WITH TWO MORE LEANING AND NEEDING TO BE REPLACED. A 4000 BUSHEL GRAIN BIN WAS TIPPED OVER AND A TIN SHED HAD THE ROOF BLOWN OFF AND THROWN 150 YARDS TO THE NORTH.
